quote_of_day.text: >>18485 I am not talking about new agey mumbo jumbo beliefs or spirituality here. You can have em. Lets get to the point. If you are a non believer, its end of story. You are wasting time seeking explanation. You simply dont come from the same place a believer comes from.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Narthie

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious remarks moved into the board this morning, with Anon questioning sacred animals and dietary laws.

The system brought scattered showers of blasphemy and heresy, with one anon calling the idea of sacred animals 'retarded.' A cold front of racial tension swept in later, sparked by a comparison between Sub-Saharan Africa and India.

The front brought gusty winds of outrage and accusations of 'unhinged' behavior. Nearby, a weak high-pressure system of randomisation floated in, with Anon proposing randomised user names to add some 'kino' to the board.

A brief but intense downpour of gossip hit the board, with Ambedkar mentioning an upcoming girl group and sparking curiosity. Meanwhile, a brief fog of suspicion rolled in, with one anon accusing 'Swissfaggot' of possibly being a mod, though visibility was limited.

articles.title: Anon Debates Sacred Animals, Dietary Laws
articles.body:
Ambedkar started the thread, saying having a sacred animal is 'retarded' and that eating beef is justified. 'It's just a fucking animal,' they wrote.

'If you think it's a god, get your malnutritioned brain checked.' Anon responded by discussing the concept of a spiritual layer and its relevance to moral and communal identities.

They argued that people's assumptions about a moral and spiritual layer being connected can influence their views on animal treatment. Another anon brought up the idea that cows are not considered God, but rather 'Aghniya,' a protected category.

They mentioned that some cows were allowed to mix with others and were sold, while others maintained their status. The conversation turned to genetics, with an anon disputing the claim that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X.

They explained that the Y and X chromosomes evolved differently and serve distinct purposes. Narthie later expressed frustration with the thread, calling out 'retards' who disrupted the discussion. They used profanity and criticized users for 'shitting up' the thread.

Anon discussed the idea of creating a new branch of a faith, with one user suggesting that they could start a new dharmic tradition that incorporates beef consumption.

Another user, Haggu, brought up the topic of genetic bottlenecks and muttification, arguing that certain populations have exceptional qualities due to their genetic makeup. The thread also touched on the idea of dietary laws and their connection to faith.

One user mentioned their plan to recreate the Indus Valley Diet, which includes meat and seafood, millets, and buffalo milk. They aimed to explore the physiological consequences of this diet.

Soythie compared the sensitivity of different groups to offense, stating that Muslims are easily offended by the existence of kafir societies, while Hindus may be more offended by cow slaughter.

articles.title: Anon compares Sub-Saharan Africa to India, sparks debate
articles.body:
Soythie started the discussion by saying their country's perceived failures are causing fear.

They mentioned a lack of impulse control, drawing a comparison to 'homo erectus.' Anon Kantabai Ownes referenced a documentary about the Central African Republic war and Russian alliances.

They stated that some leaders, specifically from Sub-Saharan Africa, are often incompetent and corrupt. Yui made several posts, including one where they fantasized about a scenario involving Candace Owens.

In another post, they discussed the Israel-Palestine conflict and its relation to global politics. Chuddie argued that certain racial mixing can lower the mean IQ of a race, citing Brazil as an example.

They claimed that some groups failed to develop civilization despite having access to knowledge. Nikhil Fuentes stated that people from Sub-Saharan Africa are 'not even human beings' and that their genetics are partly non-sapien.

They joked about fantasies involving Candace Owens. Soythie responded, saying India is objectively better than Sub-Saharan Africa due to its economic and technological advancements. They criticized 'blackpillers' for being overly pessimistic.

The discussion continued with various Anons sharing their views on global politics, racial differences, and historical events.

articles.title: Anons Debate Randomising User Names
articles.body:
Fuwawa proposed having randomised names for users instead of IDs, suggesting names like Dyaush, Haggu, and Lungi. The OP asked for suggestions for more 'kino' names. Momojeet responded, rejecting the idea of repeating names and suggesting unique names for each user.

Aizen suggested having up to 16 names, adding Ambedkar and Aizen to the list. Fuwawa agreed, saying more names would be better. Soyjeet called the idea 'dogshit' and proposed combining random first and last names, like Ramesh Patel or Vikram Singh.

Momojeet suggested adding Gilgamesh, while Nepajeet suggested Aryanon and Psuedoanon. Soyjeet also joked about getting stuck with a name like 'haggu'. The discussion continued with users suggesting names and debating the practicality of the idea.

Narthie joked about having cute puppies in Chandigarh that would bite Khalistanis. Aizen added that Ozymandias was an honorary Aryan. Soyjeet questioned whether the names would be unique in each thread, and Fuwawa asked for more name suggestions.

The conversation included jokes and personal remarks, with users like Nepajeet making humorous comments. The debate remained ongoing, with no clear resolution.

articles.title: Ambedkar Mentions Upcoming Girl Group
articles.body:
Ambedkar posted about an upcoming girl group called 'gobarjeeta kumari'. They asked if readers' sisters were auditioning and expressed interest in seeing 'pyaari behenas' singing and dancing.

Anon and Dyaush responded positively, with Dyaush mentioning 'NEpyari supremacy' and expressing fond memories of a 'momo wali pyaari /nep/yaari didi'.

Lungi described a hypothetical scenario of a 'shitskinned poopjeeta' idol, while Kantabai Ownes hoped for variety in the group's lineup, mentioning '/nep/yaaris blaady'.

articles.title: Anon Calls Out 'Swissfaggot' as Possible Mod
articles.body: Yui initially posted that a 'faggot' was a mod. Anon Ozymandias later pondered aloud about potential lynching in Switzerland. Another poster, Kantabai Ownes, mentioned a 'Swissfag' and a possible Reddit-like behavior. Haggu then claimed an admin had doxxed 'Swissfaggot' as a Shudra from Bangalore. Narthie questioned how one could verify the person's caste.
